commands.purpose: Enumerate Branches. Filter by tenant / organization / region / status. Prints a table with id, name, org, region, status, is_default, opening_hours_summary, facilities_count. Ops tooling — not a tenant hot path.

Print full detail for one Branch: identity + address (formatted per region locale) + coordinates + capacity + amenities + current is_open_now + counts of downstream references (facilities, teams, active sessions, active memberships). --include-hours expands the full opening_hours schedule for the current + next week including exceptions.

commands.signature: branch:create {tenant : ULID or slug} {organization : ULID or slug} {region : ULID or slug} {name} {--slug=} {--code=} {--is-default} {--json-file=}
commands.purpose: CLI-triggered creation. Resolves the three parent aggregates by identifier + invokes BranchProvisioner. --json-file accepts a full body payload for structured-address + coordinates + hours; ideal for one-off imports. Prints the resulting ULID + the URL for the tenant admin to review.
Flip is_default=true on the target Branch. Observer enforces exactly-one-default per (tenant, organization) — the previous default is demoted in the same transaction. Fires BranchDefaultChanged.
Transition status active → paused. Fires BranchPaused + BranchStatusChanged. --resume-at accepts ISO 8601; the scheduler auto-resumes at the boundary via `branch:resume-due` (see schedule.json).
Transition status paused → active. Fires BranchResumed + BranchStatusChanged.

Transition status → closed. Refuses when active downstream rows reference (teams, facilities, sessions, bookings, memberships). --closed-date accepts a past-dated closure for post-hoc admin corrections. --successor identifies the replacement Branch for the closure notification email.

Transition status closed → active. Refuses when a successor Branch was created under the same (tenant, organization) post-close (BRANCH_SUCCESSOR_EXISTS, 409). Fires BranchReopened + BranchStatusChanged.

Replace the branch's opening_hours JSON blob. --json-file reads from disk (the canonical automated path). --interactive opens an inline prompt-driven editor day-by-day (ideal for support agents). Refuses multi-period days on tenants without `branch_multi_period_hours`.

Explicitly re-run geocoding. --force bypasses the address_hash idempotency short-circuit (useful when the subprocessor's canonical rendering changed). Refuses when the tenant lacks `branch_geocoding` entitlement.

Compare the tenant-supplied `address.formatted` against the subprocessor's canonical form + validate the structured shape against the country's postal format. Prints a diff report + a compliance-severity audit row when drift is detected. Read-only — never mutates the row.
